title: Leftover-Rice Egg Fried Rice

subs.name: 100 g firm tofu, crumbled and pan-fried
subs.notes: vegan version — fry tofu separately until golden before adding rice

ingredients.name: spring onion, whites and greens separated

subs.name: regular onion, finely chopped
subs.notes: cook the onion 3 minutes before adding garlic

subs.name: 1 tsp salt + a dash of vinegar
subs.notes: if soy sauce is not available; will miss the umami depth

steps.text: Break the cold rice apart with your hands so there are no clumps — this is essential. Cold, separated grains stir-fry; fresh clumpy rice steams and turns mushy.
Heat a kadai or wok on the highest flame possible until it begins to smoke. Add oil and immediately add garlic and spring onion whites. Stir-fry 30 seconds until fragrant.

Add carrot and peas and stir-fry 2 minutes on high until barely tender. Push everything to the side and pour the beaten eggs into the cleared space. Scramble quickly into small curds, then mix through the vegetables.
Add the rice and toss vigorously on high heat for 3–4 minutes, spreading across the pan and letting it sit untouched for 30 seconds at a time to develop a light crust on some grains.
Add soy sauce, black pepper, and vinegar. Toss another minute. Taste for salt — soy sauce is salty, so go carefully. Scatter spring onion greens over and serve immediately.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: egg → 100 g firm tofu, crumbled and pan-fried (vegan version — fry tofu separately until golden before adding rice); soy-sauce → 1 tsp salt + a dash of vinegar (if soy sauce is not available; will miss the umami depth); spring-onion → regular onion, finely chopped (cook the onion 3 minutes before adding garlic)
